title: melaleuca lanceolata
intro: Melaleuca lanceolata, also known as the narrow-leaved melaleuca or tea tree, is a plant species native to Western Australia and is a member of the myrtle family (Myrtaceae). The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been attributed to its essential oils. The health benefits of Melaleuca lanceolata include treating skin conditions such as acne, wounds, and minor cuts, as well as reducing inflammation and relieving respiratory issues. Melaleuca lanceolata is often prepared as a tea or infused oil, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
